help.base:
【🎲 Dice System】
╭────── 🎯 Secret rolls ──────
│ • dr [command] - result via DM
│ • ddr/dddr - DM to configured GMs
│ • .drgm - GM settings
│
├────── 📊 Basic rolls ──────
│ Format: [count]d[sides][operator][modifier]
│
│ Examples:
│ 　• 2d6+1 - roll two d6 +1
│ 　• (2d6+1)*2 attack!
│ 　  sample: (10[5+5]+1)2 = 22
│
│ Advanced:
│ 　• .5 3D6 - roll 3d6 five times
│ 　• math + parentheses
│ 　• comparisons >, <, >=, <=
│
│ Keep/drop:
│ 　• kh - keep highest
│ 　• kl - keep lowest
│ 　• dh - drop highest
│ 　• dl - drop lowest
│
│ Examples:
│ 　• 3d6kh1 - keep highest die
│ 　• 3d6dl2 - drop lowest two
│
├────── 🎲 RPG Dice Roller ──────
│ Command: .rr [notation]
│
│ Features:
│ 　• Foundry VTT notation
│ 　• Advanced roll options
│
│ Examples:
│ 　• 1d10r1 - reroll 1s
│ 　• 5d10!k2 - explode, keep 2
│
│ 📚 Full docs:
│ dice-roller.github.io/documentation
╰──────────────

│ Forward character-card or request-roll button results
│ to a channel you choose.
│

│ .forward [Discord message link]

│ .forward show

│
│ .forward delete [ID]

│

│ • Only your own character-card buttons
│ • One button → one target channel
│ • VIP level limits how many you can set
│ • Settings are saved permanently

│ • .ch button (character status)
│ • .char button (character roll)
│ • .re (request roll / click)
│
├──── 📋 Examples ────
│ .forward https://discord.com/...
│ .forward show
│ .forward delete 1
╰──────────────

advroll.game_name: 【Advanced Rolls】 .ca | D66(sn) | 5B10 Dx | 5U10 x y | .int x y
advroll.help:
【🎲 Advanced Rolling Guide】
╭────── 📐 Math (.ca) ──────
│ .ca expressions (no dice)
│ Basic: .ca 1.2 * (2 + 4.5)
│ Units: 12.7 m to inch
│ Advanced: sin(45 deg) ^ 2
├────── 🎲 Special dice ──────
│ D66 / D66s (low first) / D66n (high first)
├────── 🎯 Dice pools ──────
│ 5B10 / 5B10S — roll without sum (optional sort)
│ 5B10>=5, 5B10 D5 — count successes vs threshold
│ 5U10 8 — exploding dice on 8+
│ 5U10 8 9 — count rolls ≥9
├────── 🎯 Quick range ──────
│ .int 20 50 — random integer 20–50
╰──────────────
advroll.ca_error_footer:
Note: math only, no dice.
Examples: .ca 1.2*(2+4.5), 12.7 m to inch
advroll.slash_ca_input_required:
Input required.
 e.g. .ca 1.2 * (2 + 4.5), 12.7 m to inch
 sin(45 deg) ^ 2, unit conversions
advroll.xuy_min_threshold: Bonus dice threshold must be above 2.

├────── 💡 Notes ──────

ddr.help:
【🎲 Dark Roll GM】
╭──── 📝 Overview ──────
│ • Register GMs to receive private rolls
│ • Custom GM display names supported
│
├──── 👑 GM commands ──────
│ • .drgm addgm [alias] — register as GM
│ • .drgm show — list GMs
│ • .drgm del [index] / .drgm del all
│
├──── 🎲 Roll modes ──────
│ • dr [cmd] — result to self only
│ • ddr [cmd] — result to GM + self
│ • dddr [cmd] — result to GM only
│
├──── 💡 Examples ──────
│ .drgm addgm Host
│ dr 2d6 | ddr cc 50 Stealth | dddr .sc 1/1d3
│
├──── ⚠️ Notes ──────
│ • Use .drgm show to verify GMs before rolling
╰──────────────

coc.cult_leader_0:
Wealthy Merchant
Money is power. A successful small-business owner; a member of a multinational corporate board; a powerful film producer; an investment banker, and the like.
coc.cult_leader_1:
Family Patriarch/Matriarch
Blood ties are a special and binding connection. The ancient grandmother of a sprawling family in the Appalachian Mountains; the venerable great-grandfather of a large, aristocratic household; a descendant of a powerful sorcerer; a single parent of twins.
coc.cult_leader_2:
Gang Leader
Criminals know how to operate beneath the radar—experience that may prove useful when concealing a Cthulhu cult's activities. The leader of a city street gang; the boss of a criminal organization; the head of a major drug cartel; an up-and-coming street tough admired by other kids.
coc.cult_leader_3:
Religious Leader
Already in a position of influence with access to large crowds, including people desperate for help and therefore more vulnerable to fraud and temptation.
These religious leaders may enjoy tax exemptions. A priest, rabbi, imam, or pastor of a mainstream faith.
coc.cult_leader_5:
Politician
Political influence can steer policy and even change laws. A mayor or city council member; a state legislator; a governor or even a national president.
coc.cult_leader_6:
Farmer/Factory Worker
A "blue-collar" cult leader may hold sway over the people who keep society running—factory workers, rural laborers, construction crews, and maintenance staff.
coc.cult_leader_7:
Military Officer
A cult embedded in the armed forces is a terrifying prospect. Perhaps a senior officer in the ranks, or an operative in an intelligence agency such as the FBI or CIA—or, for that matter, a similar agency in another country.
coc.cult_leader_8:
Ship Captain
At sea, a captain holds tremendous authority. Perhaps the master of a large cargo vessel, a sailing ship, a merchantman, or even a cruise liner.
coc.cult_leader_9:
Outsider
These individuals operate on the margins of society and are often overlooked—a traveling salesman, a web developer who works from home, or a blues guitarist/singer.
coc.cult_power_0:
Mythos Creatures
Some form of association with Mythos beings such as Deep Ones, Rat-Things, Shoggoths, Star Vampires, and the like.
In exchange for service or sacrifice, the monster protects and aids the cult leader.
coc.cult_power_1:
Artifacts
Items that grant spell-like abilities, protection, means of attack, or similar effects (such as reservoirs for Magic Points or even POW).
The artifact may originate from beyond Earth or from antiquity.
coc.cult_power_2:
Technology
Perhaps from Xoth (Cthulhu's home before coming to Earth), or from another dimension or another world, such a device may provide spell-like effects, store Magic Points, serve as a magical weapon, or offer protection like armor.
coc.cult_power_3:
Granted Power
A form of "blessing" implanted in the cult leader's mind by Cthulhu or its servitors. This power may replicate the same spell effects—or diminished ones, usually at half cost—in Magic Points or POW.
The power may take other forms as well: physical or sensory changes—tentacles sprouting from the face or body, scaly skin like armor, heightened senses, and so on.
coc.cult_goal_0:
Wealth
Whether gold from the sea, financial foresight, or precious ancient relics, the cult believes Cthulhu will bestow some form of wealth. This may be something specific and useful to an individual or the group. Do not underestimate the power of money—it can drive people to the basest acts.
coc.cult_goal_1:
Magical Power
Knowledge of mighty spells and rituals. Such magic may offer valuable insight into the cosmos (higher learning), or the power to control and/or kill others—magical mastery over life itself. Revealing reality's secrets is an intoxicating, enthralling draught many wish to drink deep of. Perhaps gaining magical power is a means to a greater end.
coc.cult_goal_2:
Desire
This may mean companionship, bodily craving, or lust. The world holds many lonely people who will do almost anything to connect with others. Some are addicted to extreme pleasure; membership in the cult can legitimize their behavior and desires.
coc.cult_goal_3:
Redemption
Cthulhu's rise will bring unprecedented terror and destruction. Some believe that by appeasing Cthulhu they will survive the cataclysm as the chosen who will usher in a new age under Cthulhu's rule. What the cult does now is sell people their supposed salvation.
coc.cult_goal_4:
Power Over Others
Control over others—individuals, an organization, a community, a city, a nation, and so on. Making people submit to the cult's will is central to its mission. Perhaps through magic, brainwashing, or subtle conditioning. The cult wants people to serve it, whether to advance some plan or simply because the cult believes Cthulhu demands it.
coc.cult_goal_5:
Cosmic Understanding
Seekers of truth, no matter how horrifying or cursed. "Truth" is sometimes subjective—the more subjective it is, the better the story one can weave. Knowledge is power. Who knows what delving into Mythos mysteries may reveal.
coc.cult_goal_6:
Violence
Some wish to see the world burn. These cultists kill and harm in Cthulhu's name. They see their acts as ultimate freedom, abandoning morality to earn a place at Cthulhu's table—they believe they are pioneers of humanity's next stage. Some may use violence as a means to a larger plan; others use it as a cover for darker truths.
coc.cult_goal_7:
Finding Solutions
Seemingly benevolent goals—curing disease, rooting out corrupt authority, understanding human behavior—twisted and corrupted by Cthulhu's influence. Good intentions turned dark.
coc.cult_goal_8:
Vice/Addiction
To satisfy some addiction, whether mundane (drugs, gambling, power, and the like) or Mythos-born (magic, knowledge, transformation, and so on). An addict may be easier to control—and this cult intends to control its members.
coc.cult_goal_9:
Love
Not to be confused with physical intimacy, love is a powerful motivator for most people. A cult may exploit love for family, partners, or friends to control people. Perhaps the object of that love has been warped in some way. Perhaps the cult acts out of love for humanity, trying to "save" the world in a terribly misguided way.
coc.cult_means_0:
Sacrifice
For some purpose, cultists sacrifice living humans and/or animals. This may prove loyalty, serve as a source of power, or form part of a greater plan.
coc.cult_means_1:
Ritual
One or more forms of ritual requiring a small group or a large congregation. The ritual may serve the cult entirely, or satisfy Cthulhu's will to accomplish something—great or small.
coc.cult_means_2:
Gathering
Perhaps collecting knowledge (occult tomes), specific resources, or even specific people. The cult must gather "something" for reasons known only to Cthulhu.
coc.cult_means_3:
Service
By serving a mysterious entity—one linked to Cthulhu—the cult fulfills Cthulhu's will. In return, the cult receives aid of some kind from that being. Or the cult is already entangled with the entity and must obey its commands. Perhaps the entity has no real tie to Cthulhu and merely claims one to gain more power over the cult.
coc.cult_means_4:
Creation
The cult is commanded to build something. This may be a magical gate, a mysterious artifact, a site that can focus tremendous power, or a temple. Perhaps it is only the creation of a secret network to one day seize a town, a city, a nation, or the world.
coc.cult_means_5:
Destruction
The cult is commanded to destroy something—perhaps whatever obstructs, angers, or harms Cthulhu. This may mean razing an ancient temple, breaking a mystical barrier, or overthrowing a government or organization at odds with Cthulhu's aims.
coc.cult_means_6:
Transformation
Cultists must undergo transformation—of body or mind. Human limits cannot fulfill Cthulhu's goals, or mortals are too fragile for the world to come, so cultists seek to become something greater. This may be a new stage of human evolution, or assimilation with the Mythos, as with the Deep Ones.
coc.cult_means_7:
Hunt and Kill
Destroy Cthulhu's enemies—human factions or rival Mythos races such as the Elder Things whose actions oppose the Great Old One. Such beings may threaten Cthulhu's terrible design. Perhaps in hunting and slaying their quarry, the cult is rewarded: consuming the kill may grant Mythos power or insight, and so on.

coc.madness_rt_0: 1) Amnesia: The investigator forgets everything since the last safe place.
coc.madness_rt_1: 2) Psychosomatic Disability: Psychological blindness, deafness, or limb loss.
coc.madness_rt_2: 3) Violence: The investigator attacks friends and foes indiscriminately.
coc.madness_rt_3: 4) Paranoia: The investigator believes everyone is plotting against them.
coc.madness_rt_4: 5) Significant Person: The investigator mistakes someone present for their Significant Person.
coc.madness_rt_6: 7) Flee in Panic: The investigator flees by any means, even abandoning others.
coc.madness_rt_7: 8) Physical Hysterics / Emotional Outburst: Uncontrolled laughter, crying, or screaming.
coc.madness_rt_8: 9) Phobia: The investigator gains a new phobia (roll or choose on the phobia table).
coc.madness_rt_9: 10) Mania: The investigator gains a new mania (roll or choose on the mania table).
coc.madness_su_0: 1) Amnesia: The investigator wakes in an unfamiliar place with no memory of identity.
coc.madness_su_1: 2) Robbed: The investigator was robbed while incapacitated (Luck roll for treasured possessions).
coc.madness_su_2: 3) Battered: The investigator wakes injured; HP halved (no major wound).
coc.madness_su_3: 4) Violence: A violent rampage; targets and casualties are up to the Keeper.
coc.madness_su_4: 5) Ideology/Beliefs: The investigator acts out a belief in an extreme, delusional way.
coc.madness_su_5: 6) Significant People: The investigator obsessively sought out a Significant Person.
coc.madness_su_6: 7) Institutionalized: The investigator wakes in an asylum or jail cell.
coc.madness_su_7: 8) Flee in Panic: The investigator wakes far away, lost or on a train/bus to nowhere.
coc.madness_su_8: 9) Phobia: The investigator gains a new phobia (roll 1D100 on the phobia table).
coc.madness_su_9: 10) Mania: The investigator gains a new mania (roll 1D100 on the mania table).

character.help:
【🎭 HKTRPG Character Cards】
╭──── 📝 Overview ────
│ • Personal character management
│ • One card across multiple groups
│ • Web & chat clients stay in sync
│ • Live rolls and stat tracking
│
├──── 🔰 Quick start ────
│ 1️⃣ Create a card: .char add
│ 2️⃣ Web account: .admin account [user] [pass]
│ 3️⃣ (Optional) Allow web rolls: .admin allowrolling / .admin registerChannel
│ 4️⃣ Use in channel: .char use [name] then .ch commands
│
├──── 🆕 Create / edit ────
│ ■ Full format:
.char add
name[Sad]~

admin.help:
【⚙️ Admin Toolbox】
╭────── 🔍 System Monitoring ──────
│ Status check:
│ 　• .admin state
│ 　  - Check Rollbot runtime status
│ 　  - Show system resource usage
│
│ ID lookup:
│ 　• .admin id or .patreon id
│ 　  - Show your user ID
│ 　  - Show current group ID
│ 　  - Works on all platforms
│
│ Patreon / VIP level:
│ 　• .patreon level
│ 　  - Check your and current group's VIP (Patreon) level
│
│ Debug:
│ 　• .admin debug
│ 　  - Show detailed group data
│ 　  - Show setting status
│
│ Database status:
│ 　• .admin mongod
│ 　  - Check MongoDB connection status
│
│ System protection status:
│ 　• .admin clusterhealth
│ 　  - Check DB and cluster protection layers
│ 　  - Show degraded mode and health stats
│
├────── 👤 Account Management ──────
│ Web character card account:
│ 　• .admin account [username] [password]
│
│ Username rules:
│ 　• Length: 4-16 chars
│ 　• Allowed: Chinese, English
│
│ Password rules:
│ 　• Length: 6-16 chars
│ 　• Allowed: letters
│ 　• Symbols: !@#$%^&*
│ 
│ Must be configured in direct chat with HKTRPG,
│ do not use in group channels
│
│ Channel registration:
│ 　• .admin registerChannel
│ 　  - Register current channel
│ 　• .admin unregisterChannel
│ 　  - Unregister current channel
│
│ Rolling permission:
│ 　• .admin allowrolling
│ 　  - Allow web rolling in this channel
│ 　• .admin disallowrolling
│ 　  - Remove web rolling permission
│
├────── 📢 Update Notifications ──────
│ Enable:
│ 　• .admin news on
│ 　  Receive HKTRPG updates
│
│ Disable:
│ 　• .admin news off
│ 　  Stop receiving updates
│
├────── 🔐 System Admin Only ──────
│ Service restart:
│ 　• .root respawn [ID]
│ 　  - Restart the service by ID
│ 　• .root respawnall
│ 　  - Restart all services
│
│ VIP management:
│ 　• .root addVipGroup -i ID -l LV -n NAME -no NOTES -s SWITCH
│ 　  - Add VIP group
│ 　• .root addVipUser -i ID -l LV -n NAME -no NOTES -s SWITCH
│ 　  - Add VIP user
│
│ Patreon members:
│ 　• .root addpatreon PATREON_NAME tier=A|B|C|D|E|F [-no NOTES] [-s on|off]
│ 　  - Generate KEY on create; update only tier/notes/status, keep KEY
│ 　• .root regenkeypatreon PATREON_NAME
│ 　  - Regenerate KEY, old KEY expires immediately
│ 　• .root onpatreon PATREON_NAME
│ 　  - Enable this member
│ 　• .root offpatreon PATREON_NAME
│ 　  - Disable this member (and revoke assigned VIP)
│ 　• .root importpatreon [allkeys|newonly] [-email]
│ 　  - Upload .csv to sync members. allkeys=all keys, newonly=new keys only. -email=generate email content file
│
│ Command registration:
│ 　• .root registeredGlobal
│ 　  - Register global commands
│ 　• .root testRegistered [ID]
│ 　  - Test command registration status
│ 　• .root removeSlashCommands [ID]
│ 　  - Remove Slash commands from target group (uses current group if ID missing)
│
│ Encryption:
│ 　• .root decrypt [encrypted_text]
│ 　  - Decrypt text
│
│ Shard fix:
│ 　• .root fixshard check
│ 　  - Check all shard status
│ 　• .root fixshard start
│ 　  - Start auto-fixing unresponsive shards
│ 　• .root fixshard stop
│ 　  - Stop auto-fix
│ 　• .root fixshard status
│ 　  - Show fix status

│ Send notifications:
│ 　• .root send News [message]
│ 　  - Send update notification
╰──────────────

views.roll_readme_html:
Features
  • Many dice commands: 1d100, 2d6, 1d20, etc.
  • Custom room names
  • Light / dark theme
  • Quick command panel
  • Download chat logs
  • Multiple rooms
  • Live online count
  • Custom nickname
How to use
  1. Enter a nickname (default: Investigator + random number; use Regenerate to change)
  2. Click Join room or enter a room name
  3. Type a roll command or chat message
  4. Click Send or press Enter
Dice format
  • Basic: XdY (X dice, Y sides)
  • Examples:
    • 1d100 — one d100
    • 2d6 — two d6
    • 1d20 — one d20
Quick commands
  • Open the quick command panel from the keyboard button
  • Preset: 1d2, 1d6, 1d8, 1d10, 1d12, 1d20, 1d100
  • Up to 5 custom shortcuts
  • Tap a button to send
More
  • Quick input toggles the command panel
  • Download saves chat log (HKTRPG + room name)
  • Help opens this guide
  • Dark mode toggles theme
  • Regenerate picks a new nickname
Personal room
  • Stored locally only; not uploaded to the server
  • Bot replies appear locally only
  • Delete clears content (room stays); confirmation required
  • Cannot be undone
Notes
  • 100 messages per room max
  • Room names must be unique
  • Default room Public cannot be deleted
  • Nickname max 50 characters
  • Message max 1000 characters

More tutorials:

HKTRPG Help

views.readme_body_html:
Getting started
  1. In Discord / Telegram / Line, send to HKTRPG:
    .admin account (username) (password)
    to create a management account.
  2. Open the Character Card Manager and log in with that account.
  3. After login you can manage and edit your character cards on the web.
Adding a character card

Send this command to HKTRPG (customize name and fields):

.char add name[Character Name]~
state[HP:15/15;MP:10/10;San:80]~
roll[Brawl:cc 50;sc:cc {san}]~
notes[Notes:sample]~

After “added successfully”, refresh this page to see the new card. You can also edit existing cards here.

Basic steps
  1. Click “Select character card” to pick a character
  2. Click the dice icon to roll
  3. Click “Detailed help” for full usage and formula examples
Full guide

See the official character card tutorial (includes chat commands).
views.detailed_help_body_html:
This guide covers web UI only. For Discord / chat commands see the link at the bottom.

1. Basic usage
  • Login & select card: After login, click “Select character card”. Saved data syncs with Discord / chat apps.
  • Info tags under name: Small tags (e.g. DB, occupation, traits) appear under the name. Sources: (1) “Character details” label/value pairs; (2) non-numeric current-only stat fields (occupation, traits, DB+1D4, etc.). Up to 6 tags, display only — rolls are unaffected.
  • Stats: Use ± to adjust current values. “Edit mode” lets you rename fields, edit current/max, or add/remove rows.
  • Rolling: Click the dice icon beside a roll field. Enter a dice command; you may use {variable} (see below). No angle brackets needed.
  • Notes: Plain text. Long notes show a button to open the full text in a popup.
  • Avatar: In edit mode, enter an image URL (http/https only; no LAN or localhost).
2. Advanced (web settings & syntax)
  • Roll channel (private cards): Pick a Discord channel to forward roll results. The server must be registered for rolling by an admin.
  • Public / private: Set a card to “Public” to list it on public cards for others to view and roll. Set back to “Private” to hide.
  • Variable references: In roll formulas use {field name} to pull the current value from Stats (case-insensitive). The bot substitutes the value before rolling.
  • Roll syntax (web): Enter the dice command directly in each roll field.
  • Skill then damage: e.g. baton — one field “Brawl” with CC 49 or CC {Brawl}, another “Damage” with 1d6{db} (DB value already includes +, so not 1d6+{db}). Roll skill first, then damage.
  • Roll result toasts: Results appear top-right. (1) Hover pauses auto-dismiss and pins position. (2) Click 📌 to keep until refresh or unpin.
  • Formula examples (sample stats, DB = +1d4):
    HPLuckDBBrawl
    1160+1d449
    FieldSubstitutionRoll
    1d{HP}{HP} → 11Becomes 1d11
    1d100+{hp}{hp} → 11 (same as HP)Becomes 1d100+11
    CC {Luck}{Luck} → 60Becomes cc 60 (CoC skill check)
    CC 49 or CC {Brawl}49 or {Brawl}→49Skill check (e.g. brawl first)
    1d6{db} (damage){db} → +1d4Becomes 1d6+1d4

Chat commands (.char, .ch, .forward, etc.): official character card tutorial.

views.dlog_modal_help_html:
A companion tool for the HKTRPG Discord dice bot to analyze channel chat logs.

How to use:
Add the bot to your server with permission to read message history,
then in the target channel run
.discord html or .discord txt
The bot DMs you the export — HTML is AES-encrypted for this viewer; TXT is plain text.
Data passes through our server; skip if you have privacy concerns.
HKTRPG Discord bot
P.S. Built as a side project for TRPG session logs — search TRPG on YouTube for fun replays.
